ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for grilled beef in bamboo tube; closest_parent 'beef food product' (FOODON_03302051) used as the nearest defensible anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for bamboo-tube grilled beef; A0CKP (Beef, cooked) is the nearest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this specific Vietnamese preparation; none assigned.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 180 g, representing a single bamboo tube portion of marinated grilled beef as commonly served in Vietnamese restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, ground, 85% lean meat / 15% fat, patty, cooked, pan-broiled — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Vietnamese bamboo-tube grilled beef exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked ground beef (85% lean / 15% fat, pan-broiled) as the closest defensible analogue for marinated grilled beef. Marinade ingredients (lemongrass, garlic, fish sauce) contribute negligible macronutrient change per 100 g. Sodium is elevated to reflect fish sauce marinade contribution, estimated at approximately 600 mg per 100 g based on typical Vietnamese grilled beef marinade proportions documented in FAO/INFOODS SEA compositional data for similar preparations.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Pure meat dishes with negligible carbohydrate content (carbohydrate_g approximately 0.8 per 100 g) have a glycemic index effectively at or near zero. Per University of Sydney GI Database convention, foods with insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response are assigned GI of 0 (not testable). glycemic_index is set to 0 and gi_category to low. glycemic_load_per_serving = (0.8 / 100) x 180 x 0 = 0.0. This is consistent with published GI data for cooked beef and other low-carbohydrate meat preparations.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce marinade (estimated 600 mg per 100 g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to no more than 100 g per meal and request reduced fish sauce in the marinade, as a standard 180 g serving provides approximately 1080 mg sodium, representing nearly half the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content with high protein and moderate fat
type2_diabetes.guidance: Grilled beef in bamboo tube has a very low carbohydrate content (approximately 0.8 g per 100 g) and a negligible glycemic load, making it a suitable protein source for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions without high-carbohydrate accompaniments.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol content from beef, combined with high sodium from marinade
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should moderate consumption of this dish due to its saturated fat content (approximately 4.8 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium from fish sauce marinade; choosing leaner cuts and limiting portion size to 100 g can help manage saturated fat and sodium intake within cardiovascular dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled Beef in Bamboo Tube (Vietnamese: Bò Nướng Ốc Trúc) is a traditional Vietnamese dish in which seasoned beef is packed into a fresh bamboo tube and grilled over charcoal or an open flame. The bamboo imparts a subtle smoky, woody aroma to the beef during cooking, creating a distinctive flavour profile that differentiates this preparation from conventional grilled beef. The beef is typically marinated with lemongrass, garlic, shallots, fish sauce, chilli, and sometimes sugar before being stuffed into the bamboo tube and slow-grilled until cooked through. The result is tender, juicy beef with a caramelised exterior, fragrant with herbs and the natural oils of the bamboo. This dish is commonly served as a main course or shared dish in Vietnamese restaurants, often accompanied by rice paper, fresh herbs, vermicelli noodles, and dipping sauces. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, low-carbohydrate dish with moderate fat content, making it suitable for low-glycaemic dietary patterns. The sodium content is elevated due to fish sauce in the marinade, which is a clinical consideration for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. In Singapore, this dish is available at Vietnamese specialty restaurants and represents an authentic regional preparation from central and northern Vietnam.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Bò Nướng Ốc Trúc exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS SEA, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy cooked ground beef (85% lean / 15% fat) as the closest defensible analogue. Sodium is estimated upward to reflect fish sauce marinade contribution. Confidence is medium rather than low because the base meat composition is well-supported by USDA SR Legacy data and the marinade adjustment is consistent with FAO/INFOODS SEA compositional principles for similar Vietnamese preparations.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entry for this food category. glycemic_index is set to 0 because this is a meat dish with negligible carbohydrate content (approximately 0.8 g per 100 g); pure meat preparations with insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response are conventionally assigned GI of 0 per University of Sydney GI Database methodology, and gi_category is low. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (confirmed zero for cooked ground beef per USDA SR Legacy; bamboo tube contributes no fibre to the consumed meat). vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 (confirmed zero for cooked beef per USDA SR Legacy). Sodium elevated to 600 mg per 100 g to reflect fish sauce marinade contribution; base USDA value for pan-broiled beef is approximately 88 mg per 100 g. Halal status is marked false as fish sauce origin and slaughter method cannot be confirmed from available data; consumers should verify with the restaurant. The bamboo tube is a cooking vessel and is not consumed.
